sprint: Traveller Greeting — Allow More Time For Kids
task: A direct follow-up to the just-shipped Greeting Polish sprint: "its better than before, but we need to allow more time to kids. the text is still appearing and disappearing very fast. increase all timings by 800ms." Applied +800ms uniformly to every timing constant that governs how the greeting text itself appears/holds/disappears -- GREETING_LINE_MS (2600 -> 3400), GREETING_GAP_MS (1000 -> 1800), GREETING_END_PAUSE_MS (1200 -> 2000) -- deliberately keeping their relative proportions unchanged (the fade-in/fade-out keyframe is still a percentage of GREETING_LINE_MS, so the fade itself scales proportionally slower too, not just the hold time) rather than retuning the fade curve's own percentages separately. Scoped to the Traveller greeting's own dedicated constants only, not the shared LINE_MS/LINE_GAP_MS still used by Lumo's in-person arrival lines (Scene 7, both paths) or any Gate/Journey/Arrival/Awaken timing -- those weren't what the user's own complaint ("the text") was about. Verified via Playwright: the animation's real computed duration now reads 3.4s; the 5-line sequence's inter-line gaps measure ~5200ms (was ~3600ms, a genuine +1600ms/cycle from the two +800ms bumps compounding per line); the closing pause between the last line vanishing and Segment 2's play() call measures ~2000ms (was ~1200ms); the video still resumes with zero skip at the same PAUSE_AT_S(5) the prior sprint fixed. The existing 15-assertion Traveller/Returning-Creator pipeline suite (its own wait timeouts widened to match the now-longer sequence) plus the completion/tap-to-skip sanity checks and both goldenBuild.js suites all pass unchanged, zero console errors. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to js/gatewaySequence.js only -- no CSS touched this time, so no cache-bust bump was needed.
